column3: What percent of non-digitally mature healthcare providers do not use digital technologies to personalize care as per the survey?
column13:
Is the Healthcare Industry Digitally Fit?
Our Survey Findings

Consumers are taking charge of their health using digital tools

45% search for health information on social media channels
4 million mobile health app downloads occur/day

Only 33% of healthcare providers are digitally mature

A comparison of Healthcare Providers by Digital Maturity

Digitally Mature Healthcare Providers         Non-Digitally Mature Healthcare Providers

Use of Social Media Channels
58% use social media to offer services to customers
18% use social media to offer services to customers

Use of Mobile Channels
63% use mobile channels to offer services to customers
13% use mobile channels to offer services to customers

Use of Digital Technologies to Personalize Care
47% use digital technologies to personalize care
21% use digital technologies to personalize care

Approach to Digital Transformation
79% share a common vision towards digital transformation across senior management
84% allocate adequate funding to digital transformation

28% share a common vision towards digital transformation across senior management
31% allocate adequate funding to digital transformation

Process Digitization
63% have automated core processes
31% have automated core processes

Integrated Views of Data
84% have an integrated view of customer data
33% have an integrated view of customer data

Investments in Digital Skills
84% invest in building digital skills
36% invest in building digital skills

How Can Healthcare Providers Move Up the Digital Curve?

Define a Vision and Secure Top-Management Buy-in
Define transformation goals based on patient needs
Assess existing digital initiatives, processes, systems and digital skill levels

Establish a Transformation Roadmap and Governance Model to Drive Digital Initiatives
Identify quick wins and long-term digital initiatives
Set up a dedicated Digital Services Unit (DSU)

Promote Internal Collaboration and Knowledge Sharing to Drive Internal Engagement
Build a digital communication backbone through online training and knowledge sharing platforms
Leverage early-adopters of digital to help evangelize digital technologies internally

Prioritize Skill Development and Operational Excellence for a Sustained Digital Advantage
Invest in training programs and hire experienced digital professionals
Streamline operations and develop a single source of truth for customer, operations and financial data

Sources:
1. National Research Corporation, "Consumers Going Digital With Their Healthcare Experiences Means Providers Must Step It Up", March 2014
2. BusinessInsider, "10 Ways Mobile Is Transforming Health Care", June 2014
3. Capgemini Consulting Analysis

column3: What % of US adults will develop a mental illness during their lifetime
column13:
AMERICA'S GUN VIOLENCE EPIDEMIC

Although gun violence in America is frequently attributed to the availability of firearms, another relevant factor is our nation's broken mental health care system. Although most people who suffer from mental health disorders are nonviolent, severe mental health disorders that go untreated can increase the risk for violence. Thus, if we hope to curb gun violence, we must de-stigmatize and treat mental health disorders.

GUN VIOLENCE IN AMERICA

2/3 OF ALL MASS KILLINGS INVOLVE GUNS.

In America, a mass killing — defined as having 4 victims, not including the killer — occurs once every 2 weeks.

There were 154 mass killings from 2006 to 2010.

THOSE INCLUDE
774 VICTIMS

OF THOSE VICTIMS,
161 WERE CHILDREN

GUNS & SUICIDE

31,000 GUN RELATED DEATHS IN THE UNITED STATES

19,000 OF THOSE ARE SELF INFLICTED

5% OF ALL SUICIDE ATTEMPTS ARE BY GUNSHOT.

85% OF THOSE ATTEMPTS RESULTED IN DEATH.

Suicide is the 11th leading cause of death in the United States and the third-leading cause of death for people ages 10-24 years.

In July 2007, a nationwide report indicated that male veterans are twice as likely to die by suicide as compared to their civilian peers.

ACCESS TO GUNS

FEDERAL GUN LAWS CURRENTLY ENACTED INCLUDE:

- Prohibition of firearms sales or transfers to those suffering from mental health disorders and others
- Federal background checks on firearms purchases from licensed firearms dealers; records of which must legally be destroyed after 24 hours
- Prohibition of firearms possession in school zones, unless person is licensed or the firearm is unloaded and in a locked container or rack
- Prohibition of fully automatic weapon sales to civilians
- Prohibition of children to possess a handgun or ammunition, with exceptions for employment, practice, education and home defense

BUT GUN CONTROL IS LARGELY DICTATED BY INDIVIDUAL STATES, CREATING LARGE DISCREPANCIES BETWEEN STATES. FOR EXAMPLE, SOUTH DAKOTA HAS SOME OF THE WEAKEST GUN LAWS AND CALIFORNIA HAS SOME OF THE STRONGEST.

SOUTH DAKOTA
- No law authorizing the reporting of mental health information to the National Instant Criminal Background Check System
- No short-term involuntary inpatient commitment law
- No waiting period for purchase
- No magazine capacity restriction
- Handgun open carry permitted and concealed carry permit not required

CALIFORNIA
- Requires background checks for virtually all gun sales
- Requires a permit to purchase handguns
- 10-day waiting period and a 10-round magazine limit
- Requires reporting of mental health records to the National Instant Criminal Background Check System
- Requires relinquishment of firearms for those deemed legally prohibited from possessing them
- State has some of the strongest assault weapons restrictions

Gun control laws are further weakened by discrepancies in regulations for licensed firearms dealers, which are stricter, and regulations for private sales, which are more lax.

MENTAL HEALTH AND GUN VIOLENCE

7 MILLION Americans suffer from severe mental health issues, such as schizophrenia, acute depression or bipolar disorder.

The increased risk of violence for most mentally ill people is minimal, but when paired with factors such as substance abuse and other comorbidities, the risk is more than doubled.

HALF OF U.S. ADULTS WILL DEVELOP A MENTAL ILLNESS DURING THEIR LIFETIME.

People with severe mental illness are roughly twice as likely to commit violent acts in their lifetime.

THE FLOUNDERING MENTAL HEALTH SYSTEM

LESS THAN HALF OF AMERICANS WITH SEVERE MENTAL HEALTH ISSUES RECEIVE ADEQUATE TREATMENT.

$1.6 BILLION in state mental health services have been cut since 2009.

These cuts led to significant reductions in hospital and community services for those with serious mental illness, including cutbacks to:
HOUSING
OUTPATIENT INTERVENTIONS
MEDICATION
CRISIS SERVICES

13% OF children ages 8 to 15 live with severe mental health disorders

ONLY 20% of those children receive treatment

ONE-HALF OF ALL LIFETIME CASES OF MENTAL ILLNESS BEGIN BY AGE 14. YET THERE ARE LONG DELAYS — SOMETIMES DECADES — BETWEEN THE FIRST ONSET OF SYMPTOMS AND WHEN PEOPLE SEEK AND RECEIVE TREATMENT.

500,000:
TOTAL BEDS AVAILABLE IN AMERICAN MENTAL INSTITUTIONS AT PEAK LEVELS IN THE 1950s

43,000:
TOTAL BEDS CURRENTLY AVAILABLE

This shortage of inpatient psychiatric care makes finding appropriate care difficult, if not impossible.

IN 2011
24 STATES SLASHED MENTAL HEALTH PROVIDER REIMBURSEMENT RATES FOR MEDICAID — THE MOST IMPORTANT SOURCE OF FUNDING OF PUBLIC MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES.

REAL SOLUTIONS

"We must not only tackle gun control but we must also fix deficiencies in the U.S. mental health system, gaps that can leave many people with serious mental illness and family members without an easy way to get the treatment and support we know can help avert catastrophes."
Lynn R. Goldman, pediatrician and dean of the George Washington University School of Public Health and Health Services

New Assault Weapons Ban
Senator Dianne Feinstein has re-introduced the Assault Weapons Ban of 2013, which would ban 157 military-style weapons and large-capacity magazines.

Funding Mental Health Care
The recently passed Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act will expand Medicaid coverage to an additional 16 million low-income Americans and includes requirements to cover mental health care.

Gun Violence Research
On January 16, President Barack Obama signed an executive order directing the Centers for Disease Control to resume research into gun violence and preventative measures.

Destigmatizing Mental Illness
On February 4, the Department of Veterans Affairs released an action plan aimed at reducing stigma and improving access to mental health services for veterans. The plan includes increased outreach and screening to keep people from seeking care.

SOURCES
- www.cdc.gov/ViolencePrevention/pub/USinterpersonalviolence.pdf
- www.cdc.gov/ncipc/wisqars
- www.hhs.gov
- www.usatoday.com/story/news/nation/2013/01/16/obama-gun-violence-executive-order/1842253/
- www.nimh.nih.gov/health/statistics/index.shtml
- www.psychiatry.org
- www.samhsa.gov
- www.nei.nih.gov

- www.nytimes.com
- www.washingtonpost.com

- www.gunpolicy.org
- www.lawcenter.org
- www.journalofpsychservices.org

UNMET CUSTOMER NEEDS
Why should I pay for a toilet or latrine?
Why should I settle if I can't afford the dream?

The poor are challenging customers who would rather make do with their current sanitation situation if they cannot find a desirable, affordable product in their local market.

WEAK SUPPLY CHAIN
How do I reach poor households?
Why should I prioritize sanitation in my portfolio?

The sanitation market is highly fragmented and uncoordinated, making it difficult to develop and deliver products that consumers are willing and able to buy. Enterprises with the scale to tackle these industry challenges do not currently see sanitation or the poor as priority markets; businesses that do focus on sanitation have limited reach and product differentiation.

DIFFICULT INVESTMENT CLIMATE
How can we improve the ease of doing business in the sanitation sector?
Why should we invest in this even riskier business?

Current sanitation policies are not focused on lifting barriers to market entry. Despite the existence of government sanitation promotion programs, sanitation enterprises do not have enough information about viable markets and lack sufficient access to finance.

KEY CHALLENGES

1
Stimulate Demand
- Show consumers that good sanitation is a smart economic decision
- Design sanitation solutions that meet poor customers’ needs and preferences
- Offer flexible financing options

2
Strengthen the Sanitation Supply Chain
- Build business capacity and market intelligence
- Encourage formation of strategic partnerships to develop stronger distribution channels to reach remote customers

3
Improve the Investment Climate
- Communicate clear policies that promote improved sanitation
- Facilitate private sector provision by clearly defining the role of the private sector in government strategy and policy

column13:
TAPPING THE MARKET
Expanding Access to Sanitation for the Poor Through the Domestic Private Sector

2.5 BILLION
People across the developing world use shared or unimproved sanitation facilities

A CLOSER LOOK: $2.6 BILLION
Total estimated untapped sanitation market size in Bangladesh, Indonesia, Peru, and Tanzania (based on one time sales of improved sanitation facilities to those without access)

BANGLADESH     INDONESIA     PERU     TANZANIA

What is the size of the population without access to improved sanitation?
16 MILLION     100 MILLION     8.7 MILLION     40.5 MILLION

What is the estimated annual economic cost of inadequate sanitation?
US$4.2 BILLION     US$6.3 BILLION     US$759 MILLION     US$206 MILLION

What is the average cost of latrine / toilet pumping as a percentage of average monthly household income?
39%     44%     89%     48%

What percent of enterprises identify the poor as target customers?
62%     66%     N/A     48%

THE UNTAPPED MARKET

WHAT ARE THE FACTORS DRIVING DEMAND?

BANGLADESH
GDP GROWTH     POVERTY DECLINE     SANITATION MARKETING     HOUSEHOLD SUBSIDIES FOR THE POOR

INDONESIA
GDP GROWTH     POVERTY DECLINE     SANITATION MARKETING     POLICY SUPPORTING PUBLIC-PRIVATE PARTNERSHIPS

PERU
GDP GROWTH     POVERTY DECLINE     PRIVATE SECTOR INTEREST IN SANITATION PROMOTION     DISSATISFACTION WITH CURRENT SANITATION SOLUTIONS

TANZANIA
GDP GROWTH     POVERTY DECLINE     SANITATION MARKETING     UNSATISFIED SANITATION NEEDS

MARKET DRIVERS

WHAT ARE CONSTRAINTS TO INVESTMENT?

UNMET CUSTOMER NEEDS
"Why should I pay for a toilet or latrine?"
"Why should I settle if I can't afford the dream?"

The poor are challenging customers who would rather make do with their current sanitation situation if they cannot find a desirable, affordable product in their local market.

WEAK SUPPLY CHAIN
"How do I reach poor households?"
"Why should I prioritize sanitation in my portfolio?"

The sanitation market is highly fragmented and uncoordinated, making it difficult to develop and deliver products that consumers are willing and able to buy. Enterprises with the scale to tackle these industry challenges do not currently see sanitation or the poor as priority markets; businesses that do focus on sanitation have limited reach and product differentiation.

DIFFICULT INVESTMENT CLIMATE
"How can we improve the ease of doing business in the sanitation sector?"
"Why should we invest in this even riskier business?"

Current sanitation policies are not focused on lifting barriers to market entry. Despite the existence of government sanitation promotion programs, sanitation enterprises do not have enough information about viable markets and lack sufficient access to finance.

KEY CHALLENGES

1 Stimulate Demand
- Show consumers that good sanitation is a smart economic decision
- Design sanitation solutions that meet poor customers' needs and preferences
- Offer flexible financing options

2 Strengthen the Sanitation Supply Chain
- Build business capacity and market intelligence
- Encourage formation of strategic partnerships to develop stronger distribution channels to reach remote customers

3 Improve the Investment Climate
- Communicate clear policies that promote improved sanitation
- Facilitate private sector provision by clearly defining the role of the private sector in government strategy and policy

RECOMMENDATIONS

For more information, visit www.wsp.org/dpsp

* All dollar amounts provided are in US dollars
Some figures have been rounded; please see the full publication for more details
Enterprise data from country surveys — see country annexes

+1.202.473.4594 | cwalsh@worldbank.org

IFC International Finance Corporation
WSP water and sanitation program

THE INFINITY STONES
(central wheel with six stones and descriptions)

Space Stone
- Blue
- "Tesseract"
- Allows instantaneous travel across any distance/teleportation and can be used to create wormholes between two points in space.

Mind Stone
- Yellow
- Originally in Loki's scepter then in Vision's forehead.
- Grants powerful mental abilities, mind control and can create or enhance intelligence/consciousness.

Reality Stone
- Red
- Also called "Aether"
- Can alter reality itself; manipulate matter, turn objects and beings into other forms, create illusions or change the fabric of existence.

Power Stone
- Purple
- Housed in the Orb
- Grants the user immense power, energy projection, can destroy entire planets or massively amplify the user's physical abilities and other stones.

Time Stone
- Green
- Housed in the Eye of Agamotto
- Allows control over time: rewind, fast-forward, create time loops, see future/past possibilities and manipulate age/decay.

Soul Stone
- Orange
- Location initially unknown
- Connected to the soul and essence of living beings; allows manipulation of life and death, access to a pocket dimension for souls.

(In the wheel, each stone also lists key movies where it appeared)
Space Stone - Appeared: Captain America: The First Avenger, The Avengers
Mind Stone - Appeared: The Avengers (in scepter), Avengers: Age of Ultron (Vision)
Reality Stone - Appeared: Thor: The Dark World
Power Stone - Appeared: Guardians of the Galaxy (the Orb)
Time Stone - Appeared: Doctor Strange (Eye of Agamotto)
Soul Stone - Appeared: (location and appearance teased/unknown)

LEGEND
- Colored rings indicate movie connections and which characters are starred.
- Small icons represent cameo and post-credits scenes.
- Stars illustrate critical reception / cultural impact (visual rating icons near films).

UNIVERSES & HOLLYWOOD
Marvel Cinematic Universe
- The idea of a shared universe was developed in the comics and adapted for the screen by Marvel Studios. After breaking away from licensing deals with other studios and establishing Marvel Studios, Marvel began mapping out a connected series of films starting in 2008. The MCU mixes standalone films with ensemble crossovers.

DC Extended Universe
- DC's cinematic universe launched with Man of Steel and continued with Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice and Suicide Squad, attempting a shared continuity with its flagship characters.

20th CENTURY FOX
- Although they announced plans to bring their comic book characters together, Fox's X-Men and Fantastic Four franchises have mostly remained separate from the MCU due to film rights.

SONY PICTURES
- Sony retains the film rights to Spider-Man and his associated characters; a deal with Marvel Studios allowed Spider-Man to appear in the MCU starting with Civil War and Spider-Man: Homecoming.

THE "FALSE" GAUNTLET
- The gauntlet shown in early promotional images and trailers was "unfinished" and not the final prop. The glove seen in some footage was a mock-up used for on-set effects with visual effects teams planning to replace or augment it in post-production.

(The following small captions are placed by images of characters and scenes around the timeline:)
- Post-credits scene
- Mid-credits scene
- Main plotline
- Supporting character
- Villain / Antagonist
- Cameo
- After credits

FILM NOTES (selected callouts)
Iron Man
- Invented. Tony Stark reveals Iron Man persona. Sets up S.H.I.E.L.D. and seeds the MCU.

The Incredible Hulk
- Bruce Banner struggles with his condition; introduces key characters.

Iron Man 2
- Tony's legacy and new threats. Continued development of the Stark narrative.

Thor
- Introduces Asgard, Thor, Loki and cosmic elements to the MCU.

Captain America: The First Avenger
- set during WWII; introduces the Tesseract (Space Stone).

The Avengers
- The first large crossover event; Loki uses the Tesseract and an alien invasion threatens Earth.

Iron Man 3
- The aftermath of The Avengers; Tony Stark deals with PTSD.

Thor: The Dark World
- Reveals the Reality Stone in the form of the Aether.

Captain America: The Winter Soldier
- S.H.I.E.L.D. compromised; political thriller elements.

Guardians of the Galaxy
- Introduces the Power Stone (the Orb) and cosmic rogues.

Avengers: Age of Ultron
- The Mind Stone is revealed as part of Vision; Ultron storyline escalates.

Ant-Man
- Smaller scale heist-toned entry; introduces Pym tech.

Captain America: Civil War
- Heroes split into factions; Spider-Man introduced into the MCU.

Doctor Strange
- Magic and the Time Stone (Eye of Agamotto) introduced.

Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2
- Expands cosmic lore and character relationships.

Spider-Man: Homecoming
- Teenage hero juggles life and new responsibilities, idea of accountability.

Thor: Ragnarok
- Cosmic upheaval for Thor; sets up Infinity War stakes.

Black Panther
- Wakanda and its technology; important for Infinity War setup.

Avengers: Infinity War
- Convergence of MCU heroes to confront Thanos and the threat of the Infinity Stones.

SIDEBAR NOTES (small side-captions near character heads)
- "Starring main characters"
- "Key allies"
- "Primary antagonists"

ICON KEY (near center)
- Circle icons denote main cast presence in each film
- Smaller dots indicate cameo or minor appearances
- Arrows show narrative lead-in to Avengers: Infinity War

CREDITS / SOURCES (bottom edge)
- Data compiled from Marvel Studios, official film material and publicly available sources
- Infographic design and research credit (design attribution text - illegible at this size)
- All movie images and logos property of their respective studios

BOTTOM LEFT (by gauntlet image)
- The "false" gauntlet
- The prop used for some sequences was a visual effects placeholder and not the completed Infinity Gauntlet. The final gauntlet will be completed in post-production VFX work.

BOTTOM CENTER (arrow pointing toward Ant-Man/Avengers)
- Ant-Man (XIII)
- Avengers: Age of Ultron (XI)
- Guardians of the Galaxy (X)

column3: What percentage of business analysts work in manufacturing, finance, and insurance, taken together?
column13:
BUSINESS ANALYTICS vs DATA SCIENCE

In the age of big data, parsing unwieldy amounts of information can lead to world-changing innovation.
To understand this data, companies are hiring a variety of specialists, including business analysts and data scientists.

Who Are They?

BUSINESS ANALYSTS
Research and extract valuable information from structured and unstructured sources to explain historical, current, and future business performance; determine the best analytical models and approaches to present and explain solutions to business users.

DATA SCIENTISTS
Design, develop, and deploy algorithms through statistical programming that support business decision-making tools; manage large amounts of data; create visualizations to aid in understanding.

What Education Do They Have?

The majority of business analysts come from a variety of backgrounds including business and humanities, while data scientists tend to come from computer science, mathematics, and technology backgrounds.

BUSINESS ANALYSTS            DATA SCIENTISTS

30%                         28%
Bachelor's

56%                         48%
Master's

11%                         20%
Doctoral

Sample Undergraduate Majors

• Business Administration        • Mathematics
• Information Technology        • Computer Science
• Finance                       • Information Science
• Political Science             • Computer Software Engineering
• Anthropology                  • Technical Communications
• Economics                     • Computer Information Systems
• History                       • Statistics
• Psychology

What Skills Do They Have?

Both business analysts and data scientists are experts in using data to inform decisions, but they apply their skills in different ways, using the same or similar tools. The skills listed below describe someone who has earned a master's degree in the field.

Decision-Making
Business Analysts:
Instigate change by transforming business insights from other interdisciplinary data analysis into tangible resources for business decision-making.

Data Scientists:
Discover the opportunity in the datasets by mining data and writing machine learning algorithms to support decision-making processes.

Applied Problem Solving
Business Analysts:
Define business problems and translate statistical analysis into data-driven business intelligence that improves business performance.

Data Scientists:
Create the foundation of analysis by which an applied business problem can be solved.

Data Analysis
Business Analysts:
Research, interpret, and visualize raw data using predictive, prescriptive and descriptive analysis to make it useful and accessible for business users.

Data Scientists:
Manage and organize large sets of multivariate data using methods like linear discriminant analysis and multilinear regression selection.

Analytical Models
Business Analysts:
Understand, integrate, and prescribe solutions that use data modeling.

Data Scientists:
Experienced statistical programmers using languages like and tools such as SAS, SQL, R, SPSS, Python, and Knime.

Database Management
Business Analysts:
Define and align database requirements for various forms of data, coded and not coded, using tools like Teradata, Oracle, and Hadoop.

Data Scientists:
Design and structure databases using tools like Teradata, Oracle, and Hadoop.

Where Do They Work?

column3: which touch screen is more durable - resistive or infrared?
column5: Infrared is more durable than resistive.
column13:
HOW DOES A TOUCHSCREEN PHONE WORK?

TECHNOLOGY

There are three types of technologies widely used in touch screen phones today: resistive, capacitive, and infrared.

RESISTIVE

CAPACITIVE

INFRARED (HEAT-SENSITIVE AND OPTICAL)

PHONES

Samsung Messager Touch, Samsung Instinct, HTC Touch Diamond, LG Dare

Huawei Ascend, Sanyo Zio, Apple's iPhone, HTC Hero, DROID Eris, Palm Pre, Blackberry Storm

Samsung U600 (heat), Neonode N2 (optical)

HOW IT WORKS

Small dots separate multiple layers of material that are transmitting currents. When the top flexible layer is pushed into the lower layer, the electrical current changes and the location of impact is calculated.

The current is broadcast from the corners of the screen. When a finger touches the screen, it changes the current, and that location is calculated.

HEAT-SENSITIVE
Heat-sensitive must be touched with a warm object to register point of impact.

OPTICAL
Optical uses sensors to shoot a grid of invisible beams just above the screen. Point of input is calculated based upon the point where the x-y axis is interrupted.

COST TO MAKE

$

$$

$$$

SCREEN MATERIAL

A layer of flexible material (usually polyester film) over a layer of glass.

Glass

Glass

TOOL FOR INPUT

Bare fingers, gloved fingers, or a stylus

Bare fingers only

Optical: Bare fingers, gloved fingers, or a stylus

Heat-Sensitive: Warm, bare fingers

OUTDOOR VISIBILITY

Poor visibility

Good visibility

Good visibility, but strong ambient light impacts productivity and precision.

MULTI-TOUCH CAPABILITIES?

No

Yes

Yes

DURABILITY

Fair Durability. Screens are vulnerable to scratches and other minor damage. The screen will wear over time and need replacing.

Good Durability. Glass shatters upon major impact only.

Good Durability. Glass shatters upon major impact only.

column13:
AMERICA'S
GUN VIOLENCE EPIDEMIC

Although gun violence in America is frequently attributed to the availability of firearms, another relevant factor is our nation's broken mental health care system. Although most people who suffer from mental health disorders are nonviolent, severe mental health disorders that go untreated can increase the risk for violence. Thus, if we hope to curb gun violence, we must de-stigmatize and treat mental health disorders.

GUN VIOLENCE IN AMERICA

2/3 OF ALL MASS KILLINGS INVOLVE GUNS.

In America, a mass killing — defined as having 4 victims, not including the killer — occurs once every 2 weeks.

There were 154 mass killings from 2006 to 2010.

THOSE INCLUDE
774 VICTIMS

OF THOSE VICTIMS,
161 WERE CHILDREN

GUNS & SUICIDE

31,000 GUN RELATED DEATHS IN THE UNITED STATES

19,000 OF THOSE ARE SELF INFLICTED

85% OF THOSE ATTEMPTS RESULTED IN DEATH.

5% OF ALL SUICIDE ATTEMPTS ARE BY GUNSHOT.

Suicide is the 11th leading cause of death in the United States and the third leading cause of death for people ages 10-24 years.

In July 2007, a nationwide report indicated that male veterans are twice as likely to die by suicide as compared to their civilian peers.

ACCESS TO GUNS

FEDERAL GUN LAWS CURRENTLY ENACTED INCLUDE:

- Prohibition of firearms sales or transfers to those suffering from mental health disorders and others
- Federal background checks on firearms purchases from licensed sellers, records of which must legally be destroyed after 24 hours
- Prohibition of firearms possession in school zones, unless licensed to carry
- Prohibition of fully automatic weapon sales to civilians
- Prohibition of firearms purchases by persons subject to certain domestic violence restraining orders
- Prohibition of firearms possession by persons adjudicated as mentally defective or committed to mental institutions

BUT GUN CONTROL IS LARGELY DICATED BY INDIVIDUAL STATES, CREATING LARGE DISCREPANCIES BETWEEN STATES. FOR EXAMPLE, SOUTH DAKOTA HAS SOME OF THE WEAKEST LAWS AND CALIFORNIA HAS SOME OF THE STRONGEST:

SOUTH DAKOTA
- No law authorizing the reporting of mental health information to the National Instant Criminal Background Check System
- No statute that requires relinquishment of guns when a person is involuntarily committed
- No waiting period for handgun purchases
- No magazine capacity restriction
- Concealed carry permit allowed and concealed carry permitted on most public lands

CALIFORNIA
- Only state to track and disarm individuals who are found to be dangerous due to mental illness
- Maintains a system to report those adjudicated as mentally ill to the National Instant Criminal Background Check System
- Requires a 10-day waiting period for handguns and background check for all sales
- Limits magazine capacity
- Requires safe storage of firearms
- Has restrictions on concealed and open carry

Gun control laws are further weakened by discrepancies in regulations for licensed firearms dealers, which are stricter, and regulations for private sales, which are more lax.

MENTAL HEALTH AND GUN VIOLENCE

7 MILLION
Americans suffer from severe mental health issues, such as schizophrenia, acute depression or bipolar disorder.

The increased risk of violence for most mentally ill people is small, but when paired with issues like substance abuse and life stressors, the risk is more than doubled.

HALF OF U.S. ADULTS WILL DEVELOP A MENTAL ILLNESS DURING THEIR LIFETIME.

People with severe mental illness are roughly twice as likely to commit violent acts in their lifetime.

THE FLOUNDERING MENTAL HEALTH SYSTEM

LESS THAN HALF OF AMERICANS WITH SEVERE MENTAL HEALTH ISSUES RECEIVE ADEQUATE TREATMENT.

$1.6 BILLION in state mental health services have been cut since 2009.

These cuts led to significant reductions in hospital and community services for those with serious mental illness, including cutbacks to:
- HOUSING
- OUTPATIENT INTERVENTIONS
- MEDICATION
- CRISIS SERVICES

13% of children ages 6 to 15 live with serious emotional or mental health disorders

But only 20% of those children receive treatment

ONE-HALF OF ALL LIFETIME CASES OF MENTAL ILLNESS BEGIN BY AGE 14. YET THERE ARE LONG DELAYS — SOMETIMES DECADES — BETWEEN THE FIRST ONSET OF SYMPTOMS AND WHEN PEOPLE SEEK AND RECEIVE TREATMENT.

500,000:
TOTAL BEDS AVAILABLE IN AMERICAN MENTAL INSTITUTIONS AT PEAK LEVELS IN THE 1950s

43,000:
TOTAL BEDS CURRENTLY AVAILABLE

This shortage of inpatient psychiatric care makes finding appropriate care difficult, if not impossible.

IN 2011
24 STATES
SLASHED MENTAL HEALTH PROVIDER REIMBURSEMENT RATES FOR MEDICAID — THE MOST IMPORTANT SOURCE OF FUNDING OF PUBLIC MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES.

REAL SOLUTIONS

"We must not only tackle gun control but we must also fix deficiencies in the U.S. mental health system, gaps that can leave many people with serious mental illness and family members without an easy way to get the treatment and support we know can help avert catastrophes."

Lynn R. Goldman, pediatrician and dean of the George Washington University School of Public Health and Health Services

New Assault Weapons Ban
Senator Dianne Feinstein has introduced the Assault Weapons Ban of 2013, which would ban high-capacity clips and large-capacity magazines.

Funding Mental Health Services
The recently passed Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act will expand Medicaid and increase treatment access for low-income and uninsured people. More investment is needed to expand community-based services for people with severe mental illness.

Gun Violence Research
On January 16, President Barack Obama signed an executive order directing the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to research causes of gun violence and preventative measures.

Destigmatizing Mental Illness
On February 4, Department of Health & Human Services Secretary Kathleen Sebelius announced the creation of a task force to improve mental health by increasing early detection, expanding access to care and reducing stigma. Eliminating stigma and increasing access to care will keep people from seeking help.

diginate presents
A brief history of printing

WOODBLOCKS
Stamps carved into blocks of wood.
~200

How To Use Woodblocks

PRINTING PRESS
~1440
The Gutenberg machine - A European innovation

For the first time in history the Bible was printed.
A Gutenberg Press with three basic components...

Movable Type
Composing Stick
means no more hand copying

LITHOGRAPHY
1796
Based on the immiscibility of oil and water, it leaves your fingers greasy

A modern form uses flexible machines

ROTARY PRESS
1843
The largest, fastest, biggest and most printing machine to date

The first newspaper to use it was The Baltimore Sun.

OFFSET PRINTING
1875
Colours are separated and then 'offset' - the quality achievable with this printer is outstanding

Colours are separated into Cyan, Magenta, Yellow and Black

SCREEN PRINTING
1910
One of the cheapest printing techniques, it could transfer a design onto virtually anything

Screen printing is fairly easy, here is what you need:
A 't-shirt' design
A silk-screen mesh
Waterbased ink
Squeegee

XEROGRAPHY
1938
copy, copy, copy, copy, copy, copy, copy, copy

INKJET PRINTING
1951
The computer was king and so did the first inkjet printers

To enjoy an inkjet printer you'll need(2)
Electricity (power)
Ink Cartridges (or tanks)
Ink Tanks (becoming available towards the end of the century)
A computer (No need to be online, or hooked up to the internet)

LASER PRINTING
1969
A light beam that 'impresses'

What's going on inside your printer?
Negative charges are attracted to the positively charged toner
A laser burns the image onto the drum and 'fixes' the toner to the paper
The drum rolls and transfers the toner to the paper which is then fused by heat

3D PRINTING
1984
The design exits the boundaries of the canvas!

It uses coloured plastic filaments as ink (2)

FUTURE TECHNOLOGIES
2117
some plausible future printers

The Perfume Printer
(Personalised scents - anyone?)

The Edible Printer
(Bespoke high-resolution for all ages)

The Pesto Stamp
(prints tasty sauces and spreads)

THE INFINITY STONES

THE INFINITY STONES

SPACE STONE
(TESSERACT)
- Teleportation and control of space
- Opens wormholes, enables instantaneous travel across the universe
- Appears in: Captain America: The First Avenger, The Avengers, Thor, and more

MIND STONE
- Controls thoughts and emotions
- Originally housed in Loki's scepter, later embedded in Vision's forehead
- Appears in: The Avengers, Avengers: Age of Ultron, etc.

REALITY STONE
(AETHER)
- Alters reality and physical laws
- Can transmute matter and create illusions
- Appears in: Thor: The Dark World

POWER STONE
(ORB)
- Grants immense strength and energy projection
- Capable of destroying entire planets if uncontained
- Appears in: Guardians of the Galaxy

TIME STONE
(EYE OF AGAMOTTO)
- Manipulates time: rewinds, fast-forwards, loops time
- Wielded by Sorcerer Supreme
- Appears in: Doctor Strange

SOUL STONE
- Controls life and death, the essence of the soul
- Location and powers largely unknown prior to Infinity War

CENTRAL RING (around The Infinity Stones)

THE "FALSE" GAUNTLET
- Prop versions of the Infinity Gauntlet used during filming vary from light, flexible replicas to heavier metal props with removable stones. Multiple gauntlets were created for different shots including one that fires a beam for practical effects and another designed for close-ups and stunt work.

BOTTOM TIMELINE HIGHLIGHTS
- ANT-MAN (XII) 2015
- AVENGERS: AGE OF ULTRON (XI) 2015
- GUARDIANS OF THE GALAXY (X) 2014
- CAPTAIN AMERICA: CIVIL WAR (XIII) 2016
- DOCTOR STRANGE (XIV) 2016
- ANT-MAN (XII) 2015

UNIVERSES & HOLLYWOOD

MARVEL CINEMATIC UNIVERSE
- The MCU is the most famous shared cinematic universe, launched by Marvel Studios in 2008 with Iron Man. The films are interlinked with post-credit scenes and crossover characters culminating in large ensemble films like The Avengers and Avengers: Infinity War. The MCU is divided into phases, each with its own arc and connective tissue.
- With over 18 films and counting, the MCU continues to expand into TV, streaming, and tied media.

DC EXTENDED UNIVERSE
- DC Films/Warner Bros.' attempt to create a cinematic universe based on DC Comics characters. Launched with Man of Steel, followed by Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice and Suicide Squad. The universe has had a turbulent critical and audience reception, leading to a patchwork of solo films and ensemble attempts.
- DC has also produced separate successful standalone films and animated universes.

20TH CENTURY FOX
- During this period Fox owned film rights to many Marvel characters including the X-Men and Fantastic Four. These films existed in a separate continuity from the MCU and had crossovers within their own franchise.

SONY PICTURES
- Sony retains film rights to Spider-Man characters and has produced its own Spider-Man related films. A deal with Marvel/Disney allowed the MCU to use Spider-Man in shared films starting with Captain America: Civil War and continuing through Spider-Man: Homecoming.

SIDE NOTES & ANNOTATIONS
- Nick Fury and SHIELD activities connect several films across phases.
- The Tesseract, Scepter, Aether, Orb, Eye of Agamotto, and unknown Soul Stone are repeatedly referenced and sought after in the MCU narrative.
- Supporting character arcs and smaller standalone films build towards larger crossover events.

- Infographic compiled with reference to official film releases, Marvel Studios material, and public character information.
- Art and design credits not shown here.

- Phase One, Phase Two, Phase Three labeling the three major MCU eras
- Film release years listed under each title when space permitted
- Small circular portraits indicating primary cast for each film

column3: What is the fifth tip?
column4: ["share stories of other donors' generosity before asking"]
column5: SHARE STORIES OF OTHER DONORS' GENEROSITY BEFORE ASKING
column13:
4
SCIENCE OF GIVING STUDIES
FUNDRAISERS CAN LEARN FROM

ON TANGIBILITY

REAL WORLD EXPERIMENT WITH TANGIBLE/INTANGIBLE CHARITIES

In a 2010 study, one group of potential donors read about an organization that was inherently tangible and the other read about an organization that was inherently intangible. The responses to each organization was measured.

Intangible Charity
Tangible Charity

DONATIONS TO CHARITY

Intangible Charity Description
- NO GEOGRAPHIC FOCUS
- NO SPECIFIC MENTION OF HOW $ IS USED
- NO DESCRIPTION OF PROBLEM

Tangible Charity Description
- GEOGRAPHIC FOCUS
- SPECIFIC ON HOW $ IS USED
- DESCRIBES PROBLEM BEING SOLVED

DONORS GAVE ALMOST THREE TIMES MORE TO THE TANGIBLE CHARITY

ON TANGIBILITY

REAL WORLD EXPERIMENT WITH TANGIBLE/INTANGIBLE PROJECTS

In this study, the charity remained the same (Oxfam) but the focus of the appeal to donors was different. One appeal was framed with very specific information on how the donation would be used and where while the other was more broad and general in its description.

DONORS GAVE ALMOST TWICE AS MUCH TO SUPPORT THE PROJECT FRAMED IN A TANGIBLE WAY

Tangible Project
Intangible Project

ON SOCIAL INFLUENCE:

FIELD EXPERIMENTS IN PUBLIC RADIO

In a 2009 field experiment with a public radio station, callers were put into four groups; those receiving no social information and those who were told about another donor who made a $75, $150 or $300 donation.

Average Donations

Social Information
Control
$75
$150
$300

CITING A PRIOR DONATION OF $300 INCREASED GIVING AN AVERAGE OF 29%

ON GOAL PROXIMITY

FIELD EXPERIMENTS IN DISASTER RELIEF

In this experiment, several thousand donors were sent one of four mailings that informed the donor about a fund they could support. One group was told nothing of the progress towards the goal, another that they were at 10%, another at 66% and another at 85%.

10%     66%     85%

MORE BENEFIT
Donation rates increased when told that the fund was at 85% of the goal.

LESS BENEFIT
There was no benefit in mentioning the fund was at 10% or 66% of the goal.

SOME TIPS

CREATE REAL TANGIBLE PROJECTS FOR DONORS TO FUND

ENCOURAGE DONORS TO SHARE THEIR DONATION (AND AMOUNT) IN PUBLIC

BE SPECIFIC IN HOW DONATIONS WILL BE USED TO MAKE AN IMPACT

BUILD TOWARDS YOUR GOAL BEFORE ASKING THE PUBLIC FOR DONATIONS

SHARE STORIES OF OTHER DONORS' GENEROSITY BEFORE ASKING

THE RECAP

DONORS GAVE ALMOST THREE TIMES AS MUCH TO THE TANGIBLE CHARITY

DONORS GAVE ALMOST TWICE AS MUCH TO SUPPORT THE PROJECT FRAMED IN A TANGIBLE WAY

CITING A PRIOR DONATION OF $300 INCREASED GIVING AN AVERAGE OF 29%

DONATION RATES INCREASED WHEN TOLD THAT THE FUND WAS AT 85% OF THE GOAL

REFERENCES

All of these studies, and more, can be found in the book THE SCIENCE OF GIVING: EXPERIMENTAL APPROACHES TO THE STUDY OF CHARITY. You can purchase the book on Amazon.

INFOGRAPHIC BROUGHT TO YOU BY:
re: charity

make information beautiful

column3: In which country have solar panels been supplied for low income homes?
column13:
A 4°C WARMER WORLD
We Can and Must Avoid It

Climate change is happening — now. Turn Down the Heat is a report on the latest climate science prepared for the World Bank. It notes we're on a path to a 4°C warmer world by the end of this century. The social, economic, and environmental consequences could be devastating. Every region of the world would be affected, but the poor and most vulnerable will suffer the most. This is not inevitable, however — with sustained policy action and climate-smart investments, warming can still be held below 2°C.

Human impact on the climate system is unequivocal.

Ice Age
Since the last Ice Age to pre-industrial times (thousands of years ago)

Pre-industrial Times
(around 1750)

The global mean temperature rose by 4–5°C

The same warming is taking place from pre-industrial times to 2100 (only about 300 years).

Pre-industrial Times
(around 1750) → Today

Sea levels have risen 15–20 cm.

The global mean temperature is 0.8°C higher

Ice sheets in both Greenland and the Antarctic are losing mass, and are accelerating.

Since 2002, 13 extreme weather events like floods, heatwaves, and hurricanes have been linked to man-made change.

Today → Future

If present emission trends continue, the world is on a path to a 4°C warmer world.

Likely sea level rise: 0.5–1 meter

By 2100:
4° warmer

What could a 4°C warmer world look like?
Warming is likely to have more severe impacts on the tropics.

There would be larger impacts on agriculture and ecosystems.

Drought and aridity would likely increase in tropical developing countries.

Flooding and rising sea-level hotspots

Sea level rise is likely to be 15–20% higher in the tropics than the global mean.

Corals Driven to Extinction
Current
0.8°C warmer — Threatened with increased bleaching
1–2°C warmer — Growth slows down
2–4°C warmer — Begin to dissolve
4°C warmer — Extinction possible

Amazon Rainforest Dieback
Current — Home to rich biodiversity
1°C warmer — Forest fires could double
2°C warmer — Forests could be heavily impacted
4°C warmer — Large sections transform into savanna

Water Resources Threatened
Current — Mean annual run-off decreases by 10–40% in the Danube, Mississippi, Amazon, and Murray-Darling Basins
2–4°C warmer — All above changes likely to double

What can we do?
The steps we take today can help countries transition to low carbon and climate resilient growth paths for the future.

WE MUST:
Make economic growth green and inclusive
Scale up affordable technology solutions
Invest in climate-smart infrastructure

Increasing finances to fight climate change

World Bank lending in 2012:
$7.1B
Mitigation

$4.6B
Adaptation

Making development climate–smart

Agriculture
Kenya
First soil carbon project
Higher yields, greater resilience, and lower emissions on 450,000 hectares

Transport
Nigeria
Green urban transport in Lagos
Bus Rapid Transport (BRT) system used by ~200,000 commuters/day

Energy
Bangladesh
Solar panels for off-grid homes
1.4 million low-income rural homes have electricity

Water
China
Integrating water resource management with pollution control
20 million+ people benefit

Policy
India
Development Policy Loan
Launching transformative actions in energy, water, industry, and tourism in Himachal Pradesh

Risk Analysis
Vietnam
Decision-making under uncertainty
Helping policy-makers make the right decisions on infrastructure in Ho Chi Minh

To learn more, go to climatechange.worldbank.org
Visit the World Bank on Facebook, and follow us on twitter @wbclimatechange

Source: Turn Down the Heat: Why a 4°C warmer world must be avoided

Using
DISINFECTING WIPES
at home & school

Types of Antimicrobials

Sanitizers
Reduce bacterial numbers on surfaces to a safe level.

Disinfectants
Kill bacteria, fungi, and some viruses on surfaces.

Antiseptics/Drugs
Antiseptics like hand sanitizers treat or prevent diseases on living things. They are not pesticides.

Microbes
Bacteria
Fungi
Viruses
Spores*
*Most wipes do not kill spores

What is dwell time?
-The contact time (seconds or minutes) the product needs to stay on a surface to kill microbes
-The surface should be visibly wet for the entire dwell time
-Time may change based on the microbe and purpose (sanitize vs. disinfect)

Where can they be used?
-Specific locations listed only on the label
-Use sites and dwell time vary by location and purpose

Household surfaces
Medical
Public Health

What are they?
- Antimicrobials are intended to kill or slow the growth of microbes
-Disinfectant wipes are disposable for hard surfaces like counters and tables
-Surface cleaners like wipes are not intended for use on skin
-Disinfectant wipes may LOOK similar to hand wipes. Read the label! Look for phrases like "disinfecting", "antibacterial", or "kills germs, viruses, and bacteria". When in doubt, call NPIC: 800-858-7378

Use in schools and daycares
-Only adults should use cleaning wipes – children should NOT use wipes
-Keep out of reach of children
-Follow the dwell time listed on the label
-Leave wipes in the original container
-Use the right product for each situation
-Parents: ask how children are involved in classroom cleaning

These wipes are NOT for:
Food contact
Produce
Hand wipes
Baby wipes

What is antimicrobial resistance?
If similar products are used repeatedly, they may not be as effective over time. This resistance can occur naturally, but overuse or misuse of antimicrobial products increases the chances. Follow the label and dwell time to reduce the risk of resistance.

How do I minimize risk?
-Always follow the label, including use sites and dwell times
-Never mix antimicrobial or cleaning products
-You can wear gloves if you prefer, always check the label first
-Wash hands after use
-Avoid touching wet surfaces after use
-Follow first aid instructions
-Open windows or use fans
-Store wipes out of reach of children
-Removing dirt and food from surfaces before helps wipes work
-Misuse or overuse of wipes can lead to antimicrobial resistance

Where can I get more information?
npic.orst.edu
npic@ace.orst.edu
800.858.7378

Sources
Antimicrobials Topic Fact Sheet - NPIC
Daycare and School Poison Safety - NPIC and AAPCC

column3: What hasn't surpassed email marketing by a long shot?
column13:
EMAIL MARKETING

VS

SOCIAL MEDIA

There is a heated debate inside marketing circles right now about whether email marketing or social media is better.

While there are many valid and profitable reasons for adding social media marketing (SMM) to your toolbox, the current research shows that it would be a costly mistake to toss email marketing out just yet.

EMAIL MARKETING  VS  SOCIAL MEDIA

Social Media Marketing

HAS NOT SURPASSED email marketing by a long shot.

When someone 'likes' a Facebook post or follows a company on Twitter, the context is typically

MORE INDIRECT AND LESS OVERTLY COMMERCIAL.

For all the allure of SMM, the bottom line is that

EMAIL IS STILL CONVERTING BETTER for a variety of reasons

Social media is more geared toward

SHARING STATUS

UPDATES WITH FRIENDS and engaging the way one would around a water cooler or dinner party.

Research shows that spending on email marketing campaigns

INCREASED BY 60% IN 2012, which resulted in a 4,000% return on investment!

When someone opts in to an email list it is typically in the context of an interest

TO LEARN MORE ABOUT PRODUCT OR SERVICE.

As a result, the relationship with an email subscriber can have a commercial intention from the start.

Think of your email list as your traffic on demand source,

whenever you want to send out a campaign they are JUST A MOUSE CLICK AWAY.

To be effective in social media marketing, it is generally agreed that a less direct,

MORE ENTERTAINING APPROACH that respects the nature of why people visit sites like Facebook works the best.

PEOPLE ON FACEBOOK LIKE TO STAY ON FACEBOOK.

so try sending them to a fan page instead of your home page.

Most social media can now run

LASER TARGETED AD CAMPAIGNS to people who are ideal for your product or services

Capture the people who are interested in your Facebook ads to an email list by

INCORPORATING AN OPT-IN FORM INSIDE YOUR CAMPAIGN.

Social media channels are

MORE COMPLEMENTARY and additive to email than displacing.

The key is to recognize that both

EMAIL & SOCIAL MEDIA

are distinct and different marketing channels that

CAN BOTH BE EFFECTIVE

for your business if you design your campaigns to leverage the strengths of each.

For more information on Email Marketing vs. Social Media visit www.emaildelivered.com/email-social-media

Source:
emaildelivered.com/email-social-media

EMAIL DELIVERED

column3: Who is the only wicket keeper in the world who has taken a wicket?
column13:
He studied at DAV Jawahar Vidya Mandir where he was keenly interested in football and badminton

Mahendra Singh Dhoni was born on July 7, 1981 at Ranchi, Jharkhand located in the east corner of India

He was the goalkeeper of his football team and was able to play for the district and state level. But on the insistence of his football coach, he tried out to play cricket

He entered into a local cricket club called the Commando cricket club where he played from 1995-1998

His improved batting and keeping performances led him to be selected to the Bihar Ranji Trophy squad for the 1999/00 season

In the 1997/98 season, his superb performances in the Vinod Mankad Under-16 Championship attracted the selector's eye

He made his debut against the Assam team by scoring an unbeaten score of 68

In 2003, he was selected to the India A team, which went to tour Kenya to play a tri-series tournament with Pakistan as the third team

One-Day Internationals

He was run out for a duck but in his 5th ODI match against Pakistan, he scored 148 off 123 balls to be called a match winning player in the Indian squad

In the series he scored 362 runs at an average of 72.40 with back to back centuries against Pakistan

He won the 'Man of the Series' award for top scoring in the tournament with 346 runs

He was made the captain of the Indian T20 squad in September 2007

One-Day Internationals

He was made the captain of the ODI team in 2007 and the Test team in 2008

He immediately made impact as he led the team to win the inaugural World Cup T20 in South Africa

1998 Runs in 21 innings

In 2009, he scored 1198 runs in 24 innings at an average of 70.43 to be the highest scorer of the year in ODI's

Only a few players have been wicket- keeper Captains for their team for a long time. Dhoni ranks at no.1 beating Gary Alexander by over 15 who ranks second in that list

highest scoring wicket-keeper

He's not just a batsman and a wicket keeper, but our Captain has proven himself as a bowler too. He took the wicket of Travis Dowlin of West Indies

He is the highest scoring wicket-keeper captain on the world in a Test. He's the only wicket keeper in the world who has taken a wicket

He has been awarded the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna which is the highest sports honor given by the government of India in the year 2008

He advertises more than 15 products as of 2013 and is one of the most marketable players in the world

Dhoni is the successful captain that India ever had. He led India to 27 Test match victories in 60 Tests and 107 ODI wins in 194 ODIs as a captain

Win 40 T20 matches

In June 2013, he was reported to be the 16th highest paid sportsperson in the world with yearly earnings of $31 million

He captained 70, T20 matches and he had 40 wins in his account

In Indian Premier League, he signed up with Chennai Super Kings in 2008, and in the last six seasons, has won the trophy twice. He's the only captain to have achieved that feat

In February of 2015, the couple welcomed a baby girl named Ziva

In 2010, Mahi married Sakshi, his childhood sweetheart

2016 IPL

Now Dhoni Captaining Rising Pune Supergiants in 2016 IPL

On 4th January, 2017, Dhoni stepped down as captain of India's limited overs teams ahead of the ODIs and T20s against England, on 6th Jan 2017 Virat Kohli became the new captain

He will definitely be remembered as one of the most successful Indian captains in the history of the sport

He took the decision in order to give the younger players a chance to lead the side to the next level

'M.S. Dhoni: The Untold Story' is a Bollywood biographical film directed by Neeraj Pandey, starring by Sushant Singh Rajput based on the life of Mahendra Singh Dhoni

The estimate earning of MS Dhoni is approximately Rs. 25 crore per annum

THE BRUTAL ORIGINS

-- The tradition of Christmas is highly regarded as a celebration of the birth of Jesus Christ. Although popular belief states that Jesus was born on December 25th, The New Testament gives no specific date or year of his birth.

-- Roman pagans first introduced the holiday of "Saturnalia" which was a week-long period of lawlessness celebrated between Decem- ber 17 - 25. During this period, Roman courts were closed and Roman authorities stated that no one could be punished for breaking many of Rome's common laws.

-- The festival began when Roman authorities chose a random victim to represent "The Lord of Misrule." At the end of the festival on December 25th, the innocent man or woman would be brutally mur- dered. By doing so, Romans believed they were destroying the "forces of darkness."

-- In the 4th century C.E., Christianity adopted the "Saturnalia" festival in attempts to convert large amounts of people to Christianity. The problems were - there was nothing intrinsically Christian about the festival. To fix this, Christian leaders claimed Saturnalia's final day, December 25th, was the official birth date of Jesus Christ.

...and Christmas was born.

COVID-19
Coronavirus Disease 2019

BE AWARE.

COVID-19 is a new respiratory illness that was first discovered in Wuhan, China. It is transmitted from person to person.

SYMPTOMS*

COMMON SYMPTOMS
RUNNY NOSE
COUGH
SORE THROAT

SEVERE CASES
HIGH FEVER
PNEUMONIA
SEVERE RESPIRATORY DISEASE

*Symptoms may appear 1 to 12 days following exposure to the virus

Who is most at risk of becoming seriously ill?
• People over age 60
• People with pre-existing conditions such as diabetes and heart disease

How is it transmitted?
• Through close contact with an infected person
• By an infected person coughing or sneezing
• By touching contaminated objects or surfaces and then touching your mouth, nose or eyes

Currently there is no vaccine or specific treatment. We can only treat the symptoms.
Serious cases may require oxygen and ventilatory support.

PREPARE.

Make sure you get your information from a reliable source.

Wash your hands regularly with soap and water or use an alcohol-based gel.

Cover your mouth with the inside of your elbow when you cough or sneeze or use a tissue and dispose of used tissue immediately and wash your hands.

ACT.

Avoid close contact with people who have flu-like symptoms.

Avoid touching your eyes, nose or mouth with unwashed hands.

Avoid sharing cups, plates or other personal items and disinfect all surfaces that are touched frequently.

If you have traveled to areas where COVID-19 is circulating or have been in contact with someone who has it and you experience fever, cough or difficulty breathing, seek medical attention immediately. Do not self-medicate.

Note: Information may change when more is known about the disease.
February 2020.

World Health Organization
Americas

column3: How many points are listed as the downside of adding co-workers as friends on social media?
column13:
Social Media Guidelines

for Students and Job Seekers

Social media plays a big role in young people's lives. But how much of a role should social media play when it comes to educational or professional relationships?

01 January

More than 75% of U.S. college students and young professionals check their Facebook page at least once a day.

Young Professionals in the U.S. Who Have Friended or Followed a Manager or Coworker

Facebook  Twitter

33%   40%   40%   24%   4%   20%   23%   16%

Neither colleagues or managers
Only colleagues at work
Only their manager at work
Both managers and colleagues

Quick Stats

Most young professionals appear comfortable with connecting with colleagues on social networks.

36% of young employees aged 18-29 list an employer affiliation on facebook.

16
The average number of colleagues young employees are friends with on facebook.

Who Should You Friend?

Should young professionals and students add employers?

Make this decision carefully - find out if other coworkers are friends with your manager first.

Pro
Personalized networking can be an asset when furthering your career.

Cons
Some employers may be uncomfortable to connect online, as they may have more rigid ideas of friendship on social networks.

Unprofessional activity online may impact your job if seen by your boss.

Also consider:

If you receive a friend request from a superior and want to accept, first clean up your profile and limit what he can see.

You may want to wait on friending a superior until they decide to friend you first.

If you receive a friend request from a superior and don't want to accept it, either ignore it or politely explain that you try to keep your personal and professional lives separate.

Remember to never post work-place complaints online.

Should students add:

It's a great way to make friends and connect outside of class.

You can connect with others for homework help if you need it.

Classmates

Some students may not add you, but don't take this personally.

Some faculty use social networks to expand on classroom discussions or to provide homework help.

Friending a teacher could lead to better learning and educational opportunities.

Faculty

Some view teacher-student interactions on social media as inappropriate.

Should young professionals add:

Coworkers

Can help build inter-office relationships.

Connecting online may make balancing personal life and professional life difficult.

May lead to gossip and other inter-office problems.

Competitors

Friending competitors can show that you support and appreciate their accomplishments.

Your competitor may learn company secrets from you.

Your current employer may think you want to leave.

Control who can see your posts and information by customizing privacy settings.

Don't make it personal if someone decides not to friend you back.

More Options

Don't friend coworkers or associates you don't trust.

Sources: cisco.com | moneyland.time.com | hrcommunication.com | mainstreet.com | education.com | kavaliro.com | cbsnews.com | careerrookie.com | youtube.com | wikihow.com | huffingtonpost.com | abc2news.com

Information provided by: http://www.onlinedegrees.org/

column3: which touch screen technology is more costlier to make - resistive or infrared?
column13:
HOW DOES A
TOUCHSCREEN
PHONE WORK?

TECHNOLOGY

There are three types of technologies widely used in touch screen phones today: resistive, capacitive, and infrared.

RESISTIVE     CAPACITIVE     INFRARED (HEAT-SENSITIVE AND OPTICAL)

PHONES
Samsung Messager Touch, Samsung Instinct, HTC Touch Diamond, LG Dare
Huawei Ascend, Sanyo Zio, Apple's iPhone, HTC Hero, DROID Eris, Palm Pre, Blackberry Storm
Samsung U600 (heat), Neonode N2 (optical)

HOW IT WORKS
Small dots separate multiple layers of material that are transmitting currents. When the top flexible layer is pushed into the lower layer, the electrical current changes and the location of impact is calculated.
The current is broadcast from the corners of the screen. When a finger touches the screen, it changes the current, and that location is calculated.
HEAT-SENSITIVE
Heat-sensitive must be touched with a warm object to register point of impact.
OPTICAL
Optical uses sensors to shoot a grid of invisible beams just above the screen. Point of input is calculated based upon the interruption where the x-y axis is interrupted.

COST TO MAKE
$
$$
$$$

SCREEN MATERIAL
A layer of flexible material (usually polyester film) over a layer of glass.
Glass
Glass

TOOL FOR INPUT
Bare fingers, gloved fingers, or a stylus
Bare fingers only
Optical: Bare fingers, gloved fingers, or a stylus
Heat-Sensitive: Warm, bare fingers

OUTDOOR VISIBILITY
Poor visibility
Good visibility
Good visibility, but strong ambient light impacts productivity and precision.

MULTI-TOUCH CAPABILITIES?
No
Yes
Yes

DURABILITY
Fair Durability. Screens are vulnerable to scratches and other minor damage. The screen will wear over time and need replacing.
Good Durability. Glass shatters upon major impact only.
Good Durability. Glass shatters upon major impact only.

Pinterest

Key differentiating trait:
More of a niche platform, despite very large usage. Great for businesses that involve planning, design, creation, making. Heavily female audience.

Link clicks

High

Twitter

Fast-moving, real-time, news-driven platform. Strong customer service element, be prepared to engage and respond to customers here.

Best for:
Link clicks, engagement, and foot traffic

Resource and expertise level needed:
Low

Good for these business types:
Retail
Nonprofits
Newspapers & media outlets

LinkedIn

Key differentiating trait:
Unlike almost every other platform, which is designed for the user to focus on their personal interests, this is a profession-driven platform.

Best for:
Link clicks, event registrations

Resource and expertise level needed:
Medium

Good for these business types:
Luxury goods & services
Software providers
B2B Companies

Instagram

Key differentiating trait:
There are very few ways to link from Instagram, so this is a brand-building platform. You need to have lots of high-quality images and videos to support this platform.

Best for:
Foot traffic, word-of-mouth awareness, engagement

Resource and expertise level needed:
Medium to high

Good for these business types:
Bakeries & Coffee Shops
Travel agencies
Art museums

Key differentiating trait:
This is the platform, but it doesn't mean you need to be there. With the recent challenges the platform has faced, some users are choosing to spend their social media time elsewhere (like Instagram, which is also a Facebook platform). That being said, there's still no better place to find and engage customers, donors, or others.

Best for:
Link clicks, word-of-mouth awareness, event registrations

Resource and expertise level needed:
Low

Good for these business types:
Local Brick & Mortar Stores
Nonprofits
B2B Companies

RISE OF THE DRONES

Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are remote-controlled aircraft that can carry cameras, sensors and weapons over enemy territory. The decade since 9/11 has seen these remote aircraft increase in prominence from speculative prototypes to America's primary counterterrorism weapon.

The largest drone...
With a range of 10,000 miles (16,000 kilometers), the Northrop Grumman RQ-4 Global Hawk cruises at high altitude, loitering over an area for up to 30 hours. The drone produces high-quality surveillance images using its suite of sensors.

Allison Rolls-Royce turbofan engine has 7,050 lb (3,200 kg) thrust

Satellite communications antenna

Sensor suite includes synthetic aperture radar, electro-optical sensors and infrared sensors

On the Battlefield: Use of Drones in Combat

1 Oct. 7, 2001: The first armed Predator drones begin flying missions over Afghanistan less than a month after 9/11. By March 2002, drones would begin providing close air support for troops on the ground.

2 Nov. 3, 2002: A CIA-operated Predator drone launched a Hellfire missile at a car carrying five al-Qaida members. It was the first drone assassination in what would become a decadeslong campaign.

3 Dec. 23, 2002: A Predator drone fires a sidewinder missile at an Iraqi fighter plane in the No Fly Zone. This is the first instance of air-to-air combat with a drone. During the surge in 2007, drone surveillance would significantly reduce the number of roadside bomb attacks.

4 May 7, 2004: The CIA begins a covert drone bombing campaign in Pakistan's tribal regions. Over the next four years, President George Bush would order 42 more drone strikes in the region. From 2008 to 2011, President Barack Obama has ordered at least 180 strikes. Some estimates put the death toll from the campaign at close to 2,000.

5 Late 2004: The U.S. Border Patrol begins testing UAVs for patrolling the U.S./Mexico border. In 2009, the U.S. begins flying drones inside of Mexican airspace in support of Mexican government antidrug cartel operations.

6 Late 2009: U.S. begins drone flights off of Somalia to monitor and deter pirates. In June 2011, the U.S. begins basing drones at the Mogadishu airport and launching drone missile strikes within Somalia against al-Shabab militants linked to al-Qaida.

7 April 21, 2011: President Obama approves the use of drones in support of the Libyan rebels. The drones destroy Qaddafi air defenses in advance of the NATO bombing campaign, and provide targeting and support for NATO allies during the war. Operations in Libya also feature the first use of drone helicopters in combat.

8 Jan. 25, 2011: A Texas Department of Public Safety SWAT team deploys a small drone during a raid on a drug stash house. This marks the first use of UAVs for domestic surveillance.

In 2009, male CEOs' took a 5% cut.

While salaries for females CEOs' rose 19%.

S&P 500 aside, in a study of 1.1 million CEOs from companies of all sizes, revenues, etc, it was found that women CEOs only make about 75% of what male CEOs make.

Where do they come from?
"Women CEOs are almost twice as likely to have been named to the job from outside, as Cafaro was, than from within, according to a Harvard Business Review study."

Still a long way to go:
Highest paid male CEO $87.1 million
Highest paid female CEO $47.2 million

The highest paid male CEO is Gregory B. Maffei of Liberty Media Corp with a pre-tax income of $87.1 million. Compare this to Carol Bartz, the highest paid female CEO at just $47.2 million.

This means that Maffei is paid 184.5% more than Bartz.

SOURCES:
bloomberg.com | thestar.com | catalyst.org
money.cnn.com | online.wsj.com

none 32.8%

None 32.8% none

15-30 mins 17.2% up to £2,039

30-60 mins 12.3% up to £4,078

+120 mins 9.4% over £8,156

When employees use social media at work

40% intermittently throughout the day

11.6% during toilet breaks

4.5% other

41.4% during lunch

22.3% at the end of the day

6.4% when first arriving at the office

Why employees use social media at work

34% use social media while at work to take a mental break from their job

27% to connect with friends and family while at work

24% to make or support professional connections

17% to build or strengthen personal relationships with coworkers

20% to get information that helps them solve problems at work

17% to learn about someone they work with

12% to ask work-related questions of people outside their organization

12% to ask such questions of people inside their organization

Who is using it most:

25 mins women

39 mins men

Employers with and without social media policies

39% with

61% without

Impact of having a social media policy

Workers whose companies have an at-work social media policy say they use social media while on the job to:

30% take a break from work, compared with 40% of workers whose employers do not have such policies.

20% stay connected to family and friends while on the job, compared with 35% of workers whose social media use is not regulated at work.

Only 16% get information that's helpful to their job, compared with 25% of those whose workplaces have no such regulations.

How employees view their use of social media

56% believe social media ultimately helps their job performance

22% believe that it mostly hurts

16% feel that it doesn't have much impact either way

6% see both the benefits and the drawbacks

The Cost of Employee Social Media

Averaged this is a cost of £2,592.60 per employee per year.

Multiplied by 30,645,000 employees in the UK, this is an annual cost to employers of £79,450,227,000 i.e. nearly £80 billion.

Factoring in lost productivity, this would cost even more.

8.3% of the working week is being lost in average.

This equates to £185,884,320,000 i.e. nearly £186 billion in GDP lost every year to the UK.

Source/notes:

• The cost per employee per year is based on a 2012 government report (HSE’s Work-related ill health and absence) and ONS median salary data.

• Hourly cost per employee is based on average weekly pay from 2012 to 2017, a national average increase of 39.1 hours, a 5-day week and a 48-week working year.

• 30,645,000 people employed in the UK in 2016.

• £2,286,400,000,000 GDP for UK in 2015.

column3: in which country has the first soil carbon project been launched?
column13:
A 4°C WARMER WORLD
We Can and Must Avoid It

Climate change is happening — now. Turn Down the Heat, a report on the latest climate science prepared for the World Bank, notes we’re on a path to a 4°C Celsius warmer world by the end of this century. The social, environmental, and economic consequences could be devastating. Every region of the world would be affected, but the poor and most vulnerable will suffer the most. This is not inevitable, however — with sustained policy action and climate-smart solutions, warming can still be held below 2°C.

Human impact on the climate system is unequivocal.

Ice Age  →  Pre-industrial Times (around 1750)

Since the last Ice Age to pre-industrial times (thousands of years ago):

The global mean temperature rose by 4-5°C.

The same warming is taking place from pre-industrial times to 2100 (only about 300 years).

Pre-industrial Times (around 1750)  →  Today

Sea levels have risen 15-20 cm.

The global mean temperature is 0.8°C higher.

Ice sheets in both Greenland and the Antarctic are losing mass at an accelerating rate.

Since 2002, 13 extreme weather events like floods, heatwaves, and hurricanes have been linked to man-made change.

Today  →  Future

If present emission trends continue, the world is on a path to a 4°C warmer world.

Likely sea level rise: 0.5-1 meter

By 2100:
4° warmer

What could a 4°C warmer world look like?
Warming is likely to have more severe impacts on the tropics.

[World map with regional impacts]

There would be large impacts on agriculture and ecosystems.

Drought and aridity would likely increase in tropical developing countries.

Flooding and rising sea-level hotspots.

Sea level rise is likely to be 15-20% higher in the tropics than the global mean.

Corals Driven to Extinction
Current
0.8°C warmer — Threatened with increased bleaching
1-2°C warmer — Growth slows down
2-4°C warmer — Begin to dissolve
4°C warmer — Extinction possible

Amazon Rainforest Dieback
Current
0.8°C warmer — Home to rich biodiversity
2°C warmer — Forest fires could double
3°C warmer — ?
4°C warmer (by 2100) — Large sections transform into savanna woodlands

Water Resources Threatened
2°C warmer
Mean annual runoff decreases by 20-40% in the Danube, Mississippi, Amazon, and Murray-Darling basins.
4°C warmer
All above changes likely to double

What can we do?

The steps we take today can help countries transition to low carbon and climate resilient growth paths for the future.

WE MUST:
- Make economic growth green and inclusive
- Scale up affordable technology solutions
- Invest in climate-smart infrastructure

Increasing finances to fight climate change

World Bank lending in 2012:
$7.1B
Mitigation

$4.6B
Adaptation

Making development climate–smart

Agriculture
Kenya
First soil carbon project
Higher yields, greater resilience, and lower emissions on 450,000 hectares

Transport
Nigeria
Green urban transport in Lagos
Bus Rapid Transport (BRT) system used by ~200,000 commuters/day

Energy
Bangladesh
Solar panels for off-grid homes
1.4 million low-income rural homes have electricity

Water
China
Integrating water resource management with pollution control
20 million+ people benefit

Policy
India
Development Policy Loan
Launching transformative actions in energy, water, industry, and tourism in Himachal Pradesh

Risk Analysis
Vietnam
Decision-making under uncertainty
Helping policy-makers make the right decisions on infrastructure in Ho Chi Minh

To learn more, go to climatechange.worldbank.org

Visit the World Bank on Facebook, and follow us on twitter @wbclimatechange

Source: Turn Down the Heat: Why a 4°C warmer world must be avoided. World Bank 2012.

column3: Which two platforms are good for B2B companies?
column13:
Social Media Platform
Cheat Sheet

Pinterest

Key differentiating trait:
More of a niche platform, despite very large usage.
Great for businesses that involve planning, design,
creation, making. Heavily female audience.

Best for:
Link clicks

Resource and expertise level needed:
High

Good for these business types:
Restaurants
Interior design
Wedding venues

Twitter

Key differentiating trait:
Fast-moving, real-time, news-driven platform.
Strong customer service element, be prepared to
engage and respond to customers here.

Best for:
Link clicks, engagement, and foot traffic

Resource and expertise level needed:
Low

Good for these business types:
Retail
Nonprofits
Newspapers & media outlets

LinkedIn

Key differentiating trait:
Unlike almost every other platform, which is
designed for the user to focus on their personal
interests, this is a profession-driven platform.

Best for:
Link clicks, event registrations

Resource and expertise level needed:
Medium

Good for these business types:
Luxury goods & services
Software providers
B2B Companies

Instagram

Key differentiating trait:
There are very few ways to link from Instagram, so
this is a brand-building platform. You need to have
lots of high-quality images and videos to support
this platform.

Best for:
Foot traffic, word-of-mouth awareness,
engagement

Resource and expertise level needed:
Medium to high

Good for these business types:
Bakeries & Coffee Shops
Travel agencies
Art museums

Facebook

Key differentiating trait:
This is the platform, but it doesn't mean you need to be there. With the recent challenges the
platform has faced, some users are choosing to spend their social media time elsewhere (like Instagram,
which is also a Facebook platform). That being said, there's still no better place to
find and engage customers, donors, or others.

Best for:
Link clicks, word-of-mouth awareness, event registrations

Resource and expertise level needed:
Low

Good for these business types:
Local Brick & Mortar Stores
Nonprofits
B2B Companies

Constant Contact

column3: What percentage of Syrian women who participated in the survey are interested in Regular and online schooling?
column13:
EDUCATION is the key to women's EMPOWERMENT

Who are the respondents?
1,006 Syrian women from inside and outside Syria participated in the survey.

Age Group Distribution

74% of respondents hold a university degree or higher.

Do they believe Syrian women are empowered?
55% NO
42% YES

In general, the higher the education level, the more likely respondents believe that Syrian women are less empowered.

Graduate Degree
5.97%     3.19%

University Degree
38.37%    27.78%

2 year associate Degree
5.04%     4.94%

High School Degree
5.45%     5.25%

No high school degree
2.26%     1.75%

The results suggest the increased EDUCATION raises awareness about further opportunities for women's empowerment

Should educating women be stipulated and enforced?
100%

100% of respondents unanimously reported that education for women should be encouraged and enforced.

What is the best way for women to get their rights?
92% of respondents believe that EDUCATION is the best way for women to obtain equal, or at least more, rights.

92% Educational Degree
7% Learn a Skill
1% Marriage

By educating women, we can empower them to give a voice to other women, and push for equal rights.

Traditional schooling or Internet-based education?
5% Internet
17% Traditional
78% Both

Women inside Syria (2.16%) are more inclined to prefer Internet schooling than women outside Syria (1.03%). Increasing access to Internet-based education can provide women in Syria with a significant opportunity.

Do they believe that their daughters should go to school, no matter what?
0.3% NO

Almost 100% of respondents believe that their daughter should go to school. Only 3 women disagreed.

99.7% YES

EDUCATION is a powerful tool, and EVERYONE should be equipped with the appropriate tools to be able to shape their future into what they believe they deserve.

The findings are based on the results of an online survey conducted by Bareeq Education and Development in May 2017, with 1,006 Syrian women from both inside and outside Syria participating. For more information on Bareeq Education, please visit www.bareeqeducation.org

BAREEQ
EDUCATION & DEVELOPMENT

The Very Important Projects fund (VIP.fund) is a youth-focused venture philanthropy fund, established in 2015 as a USA 501(c)3 non-profit organization. We invest in youth programs and start-ups, promoting education and innovation in disruptive technologies. Our goal is youth empowerment and employment. Please visit us at vip.fund for more information.
